title: Free Robux Codes 2026: The Only Active Roblox Promo Code List That Works
author: ghost
body:
Free Robux Codes 2026: The Only Active Roblox Promo Code List That Works
The future of gaming! In 2026, the Roblox universe continues to expand, offering endless adventures and creative possibilities. One of the most sought-after commodities in this virtual world is Robux, the in-game currency that allows players to purchase a variety of items and enhance their gaming experience. If you're here, you're likely searching for ways to get free Robux codes without breaking the bank. You've come to the right place! This comprehensive guide will walk you through the latest and most effective methods to acquire free Robux codes in 2026

➤

### [✅Click Here To Get ➤➤](https://www.borderclick.com/BC/media/Borderclick/Files/roblox-update.html)

➤

From customizing your avatar to unlocking special features and game passes, Robux is essential.
But here’s the catch: it can be expensive! Fortunately, there are legitimate ways to earn free Robux without spending a single penny. In this article, we'll walk you through safe and fast methods to get free Robux in 2026 without falling for scams or third-party hacks.

There is no legitimate "trick" or "generator" that provides free Robux instantly. Roblox officially states that any offer of free Robux is a scam. These scams are designed to steal your password, personal information, or compromise your account.

Safe & Official Ways to Earn Robux Robux

Microsoft Rewards:

You can earn points by searching on Bing or completing simple tasks on Microsoft Rewards. These points can be redeemed for official Roblox Digital Gift Cards.

Create & Sell Items:

If you have creative skills, you can design and sell your own avatar items (like shirts or pants) or game passes for your own games.Roblox Affiliate Program: By sharing unique referral links to games or items, you can earn a percentage of the Robux spent by users who sign up or make purchases through your link.Official Events & Giveaways: Keep an eye on the official Roblox Blog or their social media for seasonal events that sometimes offer rewards.

Samsung Galaxy Store:

Samsung users can sometimes find limited-time coupons in the Galaxy Store that can be used for a small amount of free Robux or discounts


What is Robux and Why Do You Need It?
Robux is the in-game currency on Roblox, used to purchase accessories, outfits, game passes, and more. Whether you’re looking to enhance your avatar or unlock premium content, Robux is the key to a full Roblox experience. But buying Robux can quickly add up, which is why many players search for free methods to earn Robux without breaking the bank.

1. Legitimate Roblox Promotions and Giveaways

One of the best ways to get free Robux is by keeping an eye on official Roblox promotions and giveaways. Roblox regularly runs special events where users can earn Robux by completing tasks, participating in challenges, or just logging in during special events.

Pro Tip: Make sure to follow Roblox on social media platforms like Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram to stay updated on the latest promotions and free Robux opportunities.
2. Roblox Affiliate Program: Get Robux by Inviting Friends

The Roblox Affiliate Program is a fantastic way to earn free Robux. You can share your unique referral link with friends, family, or followers, and whenever they sign up for Roblox and make a purchase, you’ll earn Robux. This is an easy and completely legitimate method to start earning Robux without spending a dime.

Pro Tip: Share your referral link on social media platforms like TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube to maximize your chances of earning more Robux.
3. Roblox Premium Membership: Get Robux Every Month

Although Roblox Premium isn’t “free” in the strictest sense, it’s one of the best value-for-money options for consistent Robux. When you subscribe to Roblox Premium, you get a monthly stipend of Robux and other exclusive perks, such as the ability to trade items and a 10% bonus on Robux purchases.

Pro Tip: If you’re a frequent Roblox player, Roblox Premium is a cost-effective way to ensure you always have Robux on hand for purchases and upgrades.
4. Participate in Roblox Events and Challenges
Roblox often hosts in-game events, livestreams, and special challenges where you can earn free Robux as rewards. By attending these events and completing certain challenges, you can accumulate Robux while having fun.

Tip: Keep an eye on Roblox’s official Event Calendar, and don’t miss out on seasonal or limited-time events that offer free Robux.
5. Create and Sell Roblox Items or Games

If you have creative skills, you can design and sell your own items, accessories, or even entire games on Roblox. The platform allows players to earn Robux by selling their creations to other users. If your items or games gain popularity, you could earn a steady stream of Robux.

Pro Tip: Consider learning how to build games or design virtual items to monetize your skills and earn Robux over time.
6. Beware of Free Robux ScamsRobux

While it’s tempting to search for quick ways to earn Robux, be cautious of websites or apps that promise “free Robux” in exchange for personal information or completing surveys. These sites are typically scams designed to steal your personal data or infect your device with malware.

Warning: Never enter your Roblox login details on third-party sites, and always stick to official Roblox channels when looking for ways to earn free Robux.
Why These Methods Work

The methods listed above work because they all align with Roblox’s official terms of service. By engaging in legitimate promotional offers, using the affiliate program, and creating valuable content, you’re earning Robux safely and responsibly. These are tested and trusted methods that won’t get your account banned or compromised.

Conclusion: Start Earning Your Free Robux Today!

Now that you know the best, safe ways to earn free Robux, you can start boosting your Roblox account without spending real money. Whether it’s by taking part in official Roblox promotions, joining the affiliate program, or leveling up with Roblox Premium, there are multiple ways to accumulate Robux and enhance your gaming experience.

Call to Action:
Start earning your free Robux now! Join Roblox Premium for monthly Robux, share your referral link, or check out the latest Roblox events for limited-time giveaways.
Donate to Earn Free Robux In Few Minutes! - Roblox Codes Step-by-Step Ultimate Guide 2026 [RRBUX]
$

title: Log bloat with "has a collation version mismatch" warnings on template1 following DB upgrade
author: sunit-salesforce
body: ## Bug: Log bloat with "has a collation version mismatch" warnings on template1 after Postgres 17 migration Following the recent platform migration to Postgres 17.6.1.121 (as referenced on the Supabase [Status page](https://status.supabase.com/)), the database logs became continuously flooded with collation version mismatch warnings (Error Code: 01000). Running ALTER DATABASE postgres REFRESH COLLATION VERSION; successfully cleared the warnings for the primary postgres database. However, the warnings for the template1 database persist and continue to flood the logs. Because standard managed users lack the elevated privileges required to connect to and refresh system template databases, this requires a platform-side fix to update the template catalogs. ## To Reproduce Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository: 1. Open the Supabase Dashboard. 2. Navigate to Project Settings -> Logs -> Database Logs. 3. Observe recurring 01000 status warnings: database "template1" has a collation version mismatch. ## Expected behavior Managed database upgrades should automatically refresh collation versions across all system databases, including template databases like template1, to avoid continuous log bloat. ## Screenshots ## System information - Environment: Hosted Supabase Platform - Postgres Version: Upgraded to 17.6.1.141 - Timeline: Observed starting July 10, 2026 ## Additional context The issue is purely a log-bloat nuisance and does not break application functionality, but it makes scanning database logs difficult. The internal maintenance scripts need to target template1 to clear out the remaining catalog noise.

title: Supabase login fails with “User’s profile not found” after GitHub/email identity mismatch
author: AhStayinAlive
body: ## Issue I’m currently locked out of my Supabase account/dashboard due to what seems like an account profile or GitHub/email identity mismatch. When I try to sign in, I get: > Failed to retrieve account information > Error: User's profile not found ## Context This started after I tried to clean up email identities linked to my Supabase account and GitHub login. I may have accidentally removed or mismatched the wrong identity. I did not intentionally delete the project or organization. The affected project contains client data, so I’m trying to recover access as soon as possible. I’m currently on the Free plan, and I understand support may be limited, but this is for a client database and I plan to move to a paid plan once the project is moving properly. ## Support status I emailed support and received this reference ID: **SU-416784** I have not received any reply or update yet. I also cannot submit a ticket properly through the Supabase support flow because it requires selecting a project, but I can’t access my dashboard/projects. When I try submitting, I also get: > Unable to upload attachments ## What I tried - Refreshing and signing in again - Trying different Supabase login methods - Checking GitHub/email identity settings - Switching my GitHub primary email back to the email I believe was used when signing up, based on a reddit post I found That still did not work. I still get: > Failed to retrieve account information > Error: User's profile not found ## Request Could someone from the Supabase team please help check my account/profile state or point me to the correct recovery path? I can provide the project ref, account emails, GitHub details, screenshots, and ownership verification privately through the existing support thread or another secure channel.

title: Storage: INSERT into `storage.objects` blocked by RLS on any newly created bucket, even with `WITH CHECK (true)`
author: danhuynh1912
body:
- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application.
- [x] I confirm I have searched the Docs, GitHub Discussions, and Discord.

## Describe the bug

On project `xgucqsejhvjkbeyomtqf`, **any Storage bucket that isn't one of the buckets that already existed at the start of my observation window is unable to receive inserts into `storage.objects`, no matter what the RLS policy says** — including a completely unconditional `WITH CHECK (true)` policy. Every INSERT for role `authenticated` against a "new" bucket fails with:

```
ERROR: 42501: new row violates row-level security policy for table "objects"
```

This reproduces for brand-new buckets created purely to test this (different names, different configs, created via raw SQL *and* via the Dashboard "Create bucket" UI) — it isn't specific to one bucket's name, age, or creation method. Meanwhile 3 pre-existing buckets in the same project (`profile-photos`, `team-photos`, `message-images`) work completely normally with the exact same policy shapes.

Once I controlled for a role-switch testing mistake (running as the connection's default/superuser role bypasses RLS entirely and gives false positives — see reproduction note below), **the actual content of the WITH CHECK expression became irrelevant**: `true`, `bucket_id = 'x'`, `auth.uid() is not null`, and `bucket_id = bucket_id` all fail identically for any "new" bucket and all succeed identically for the 3 original buckets. A policy with `WITH CHECK (true)` should never be able to reject a row under standard Postgres RLS semantics, so this looks like something is rejecting the insert before or independent of policy evaluation for these buckets specifically.

This makes it impossible to use Storage for any newly created bucket in this project. I'm currently working around it by storing an unrelated feature's files inside `profile-photos` (the only bucket confirmed to reliably accept writes), which is an architecturally wrong workaround I want to undo once this is fixed.

## To Reproduce

Run as a role with normal migration privileges (not a superuser session — see the important caveat below the repro).

```sql
-- 1. Create a brand-new bucket (name is irrelevant — tried several different ones).
insert into storage.buckets (id, name, public, file_size_limit, allowed_mime_types)
values ('repro_bucket', 'repro_bucket', false, 15728640, array['image/jpeg']);

-- 2. Create the most permissive INSERT policy possible: no conditions at all.
create policy repro_insert_open on storage.objects
for insert to public
with check ( true );

-- 3. Simulate an authenticated client.
begin;
set local role authenticated;
set local request.jwt.claims to '{"sub":"<any-valid-auth.users.id>","role":"authenticated"}';

insert into storage.objects (bucket_id, name)
values ('repro_bucket', 'test.jpg');
-- ERROR: 42501: new row violates row-level security policy for table "objects"

rollback;
```

**Control** — identical steps, only the bucket differs, against a bucket that already existed before I started testing (e.g. `profile-photos`):

```sql
begin;
set local role authenticated;
set local request.jwt.claims to '{"sub":"<any-valid-auth.users.id>","role":"authenticated"}';

insert into storage.objects (bucket_id, name)
values ('profile-photos', 'test.jpg');
-- SUCCEEDS
rollback;
```

**Testing caveat that cost me a lot of time, flagging so it doesn't cost you any**: if you skip `set local role authenticated` / `set local request.jwt.claims` and just insert as your default connecting role, it will succeed regardless of bucket, because that role is typically superuser/table-owner and bypasses RLS entirely — that tells you nothing about the bug. The role switch is essential to reproduce it.

### What I ruled out before concluding this is a platform-level issue

- Policy `WITH CHECK` logic being wrong (simplified all the way down to `true` — still fails)
- Policy scoped `TO authenticated` vs `TO public` (tried both, no difference)
- Bucket `public` flag true/false, both at creation time and via a later `UPDATE` (no difference)
- Operand order in the check expression (no difference)
- Policy created in the same transaction as the INSERT vs a separate one (no difference)
- Bucket created via raw SQL vs via the Dashboard "Create bucket" UI (deleted and recreated the affected bucket through the Dashboard with identical settings — still fails)
- Hidden/duplicate/restrictive RLS policies (`pg_policy` dump shows only expected policies, all permissive)
- `storage.buckets` RLS blocking an implicit FK lookup (`storage.buckets` has RLS enabled with zero policies, i.e. default-deny for non-owner roles — yet the 3 working buckets still succeed, so FK checks must legitimately bypass RLS on the referenced table, as documented; ruled out)
- The foreign key `objects_bucketId_fkey` on `bucket_id` specifically — compared `bucket_id = <literal>` (fails) vs `name = <literal>` (the other column in the same unique index, no FK — succeeds); looked implicated at first but stopped being reproducible once the check no longer referenced `bucket_id` at all and still failed (see below)
- A `UNIQUE (bucket_id, name)` index interacting badly with RLS — wanted to `DROP INDEX` inside a rolled-back transaction to test, but got `ERROR: 42501: must be owner of index bucketid_objname`. My role can freely `CREATE POLICY`/`DROP POLICY` on `storage.objects` but does not own this specific index (likely owned by an internal role such as `supabase_storage_admin`) — could not test further, but flagging as a possible clue
- Session/connection-level plan or catalog cache staleness (`pg_backend_pid()` returned a different PID on every single test call — fresh backend every time, no possibility of carried-over state)
- Parallel query workers not inheriting `SET LOCAL`-scoped GUCs (tried `SET LOCAL max_parallel_workers_per_gather = 0` — no difference)
- Bucket name containing the specific string "snapshots" (tried unrelated names, e.g. `waytrek_photos_v2` — fails identically)
- A Free-plan limit on number of storage buckets (checked the docs — no such limit exists for regular Storage buckets, only for the unrelated "Vector Buckets" alpha feature; also doesn't fit the symptom shape, since a plan limit would block bucket *creation*, not object *insertion* into an already-successfully-created bucket)

## Expected behavior

A newly created Storage bucket should behave identically to an existing one for RLS purposes. In particular, a policy with `WITH CHECK (true)` should always allow the insert, regardless of which bucket it targets.

## Screenshots

N/A — this is a server-side/SQL-level bug, fully reproduced via raw SQL (see above) independent of any client library or UI.

## System information

- Project ref: `xgucqsejhvjkbeyomtqf`
- Postgres version: `17.6` (`PostgreSQL 17.6 on aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu`)
- Relevant extensions: `supabase_vault 0.3.1`, `pg_cron 1.6.4`, `pg_net 0.20.3`
- Version of supabase-js: N/A — reproduced directly via SQL against `storage.objects`, not through any client library. The original real-world symptom was observed via `supabase_flutter`'s Storage client (Dart), which surfaces it as an HTTP 400 from `/storage/v1/object/<bucket>/...`.
- OS/Browser: N/A (server-side Postgres RLS issue)

## Additional context

The bucket originally affected by this in my app is named `snapshots` (created 2026-07-09 via a SQL migration: `insert into storage.buckets (id, name, public, file_size_limit, allowed_mime_types) values ('snapshots', 'snapshots', false, 15728640, array['image/jpeg','image/webp','image/png']) on conflict (id) do nothing;`). Zero rows have ever been successfully written to it by a real client since it was created — every upload attempt from the app gets `400` from `/storage/v1/object/snapshots/...`. I deleted and recreated this exact bucket through the Dashboard UI (same name, same settings) hoping that would help — it didn't, still fails identically.

The 3 buckets that work correctly in this project (`profile-photos`, `team-photos`, `message-images`) all predate my own migration history for this feature — I don't know how/when they were originally created, possibly by a teammate through the Dashboard for a different app sharing this same Supabase project. I'd appreciate confirmation of whether these buckets have some project-level metadata that new buckets are missing, and either a fix or a supported way to create new buckets that don't inherit this issue — ideally applied to my actual bucket, `snapshots`, so I can migrate off my current workaround (storing these files inside `profile-photos` instead, with an adjusted path scheme).

Happy to grant temporary read access to this project or run further diagnostics if useful.

title: Edge Functions Test panel always shows generic error for non-platform, hiding the real failure reason
author: shkuls
body:
**Bug**: On self-hosted Supabase, the Edge Functions **Test** panel in Studio always shows the same generic error — `Edge function returned an error` — no matter what actually failed (thrown exception, timeout, gateway/DNS issue, malformed input, anything). This happens 100% of the time, not intermittently.

**Root cause**: `apps/studio/pages/api/edge-functions/test.ts` extracts the error message by reading `errorBody?.error`:

```ts
error: { message: errorBody?.error || 'Edge function returned an error' }
```

But self-hosted's Kong-fronted edge runtime returns errors shaped as `{"message": "..."}`, not `{"error": "..."}`. The key never matches, so it always falls through to the hardcoded string. This logic was written for the platform-only version of this endpoint and never re-validated against self-hosted's actual response shape when self-hosted test support was added in #42350.

**To reproduce**

1. Self-host Supabase (`supabase start`), deploy a function via `supabase functions deploy <name>`.
2. Studio → Edge Functions → the function → **Test** → send a request that fails.
3. Response shows the generic message regardless of the real cause.

**Proof** — curl against the raw edge runtime vs. Studio's test-proxy endpoint, before/after the fix:

```
# the real error, straight from the edge runtime
$ curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:54321/functions/v1/og-images -d '{}'
{"message":"name resolution failed"}

# Studio's proxy, same failure — before fix (master@d5fde192d5)
$ curl -X POST http://localhost:8082/api/edge-functions/test -d '{"url":"...","method":"POST","body":"{}","headers":{}}'
{"status":503,"error":{"message":"Edge function returned an error"}}
```

Also confirmed in the Studio UI itself (Test → Send Request): before the fix shows "Edge function returned an error"; this is unchanged for gateway-level failures (see Wider fix below for why), but is now fixed for worker-level failures since the worker itself emits the right key.

**Fix**: #47836 — fixes the self-hosted worker (`docker/volumes/functions/main/index.ts`) to emit `error` instead of `msg`, matching the shape Studio's proxy already expects (`errorBody?.error`). No change needed on Studio's side once the source emits the right key — Studio's original single-key `error` check was left as-is, per @aantti's feedback below.

**Wider fix**

@aantti pointed out this should be fixed at the source rather than only patched in Studio (see [#47836](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/pull/47836#issuecomment-4944665915)). The same `msg`/`error` inconsistency existed in the other places that generate edge function error responses. Fixed to match platform's `error` shape everywhere it's under our control:

- Self-hosted docker-compose worker (`docker/volumes/functions/main/index.ts`) — fixed, part of #47836
- `supabase start` local dev worker (`supabase/cli`'s `apps/cli/src/shared/functions/serve.main.ts`, plus the legacy Go CLI's bundled copy) — supabase/cli#5862
- Docs examples teaching the same pattern (`examples/edge-functions/supabase/functions/sentryfied/index.ts` and the shared `_shared/jwt/*` auth middlewares) — #47849

Gateway-level errors (e.g. Kong routing failures, the specific case reproduced above) happen before any worker script runs, so they can't be fixed at the source. Per @aantti, Studio doesn't special-case these — they still show the generic fallback message, and that's expected.

**Known limitation**: doesn't handle nested error objects (e.g. `{"error": {"message": "..."}}`) or non-JSON gateway error pages (e.g. raw HTML from a 502/504) — flagged as a follow-up, not blocking.

title: `supabase start` reports "already running" when all local containers are stopped
author: marty-mkh
body:
After updating Docker Desktop on Windows to 4.81.0, my local Supabase containers no longer start automatically when Docker starts.

Running:

```bash
npx supabase start
```

returns:

```text
supabase start is already running.
supabase_db_<project> container is not running: exited
```

The CLI refuses to start the existing containers because it considers the project "already running", even though every container is in the `Exited` state.

### Expected behavior

After Docker Desktop starts, an existing local Supabase stack should either resume automatically (as it did previously) or supabase start should detect that the containers exist but are stopped and start them instead of reporting that the project is already running.

### Actual behavior

All containers exist but are stopped:

```bash
docker ps -a
```

shows all `supabase_*` containers in `Exited` state.

The following works correctly:

```bash
docker start $(docker ps -aq --filter "name=supabase_")
```

and also:

```bash
npx supabase stop
npx supabase start
```

This suggests the containers are healthy and only the CLI's detection/recovery logic is failing.

### Environment

* OS: Windows
* Docker Desktop: 4.81.0
* Supabase CLI: 2.109.1

### Additional information

The database container exits with status 137 after Docker Desktop shuts down, but can be started manually without errors:

```bash
docker start supabase_db_<project>
```

The issue appears to be that `supabase start` detects an existing project but does not attempt to restart stopped containers.

title: Noticed some potential issues in the repo
author: joshua-trustabl
body:
Was checking out your repo and ran it through Trustabl. A couple of things seemed worth mentioning, the scan found 12 findings, including 1 high, 6 medium, and 2 low. Not sure if you're aware, but a couple of patterns that stood out: Vercel AI tool execute() fetches a model-controlled URL and Skill fetches untrusted external content. Could be intentional, just thought you'd want to know.

---
Add Trustabl to your CI — [trustabl/trustabl-action](https://github.com/trustabl/trustabl-action):
```yaml
- name: Trustabl scan
  uses: trustabl/trustabl-action@v1
```

Title: Edge Functions returning 401 UNAUTHORIZED_LEGACY_JWT for valid legacy JWTs since today — Postgres unaffected

Body:

Since this morning (Jul 8), all Edge Function calls from our app suddenly fail with a 401, even though the session and tokens are valid and present. Nothing changed on our side, and it worked fine yesterday.

Error (client side):

{"code":"UNAUTHORIZED_LEGACY_JWT","message":"Invalid JWT"}
HTTP 401 (Unauthorized)
What we've verified:

The user session is valid: hasSession=true, hasAccessToken=true, tokenAlg=HS256, tokenRole=authenticated, and it's not expired.
Postgres / PostgREST works fine — direct table reads/writes succeed. Only Edge Functions fail.
Edge Function logs show only booted → Listening → shutdown with no handler logs, so the request is rejected at the gateway before the function code runs.
We have not rotated or migrated the JWT secret. Project is still on the Legacy JWT Secret (JWT Keys → not migrated).
Legacy anon/service_role API keys are still enabled (not disabled). No new publishable/secret keys created.
Logging out/in does not help.
At least one other user reports the identical error on a different project starting the same day (see #help-and-questions).
Environment:

Platform: Web (React + Vite)
Library: @supabase/supabase-js (JS)
Plan: Pro
Project ref: qtxbuqakivzgxxskzwwx
Region: (fill in your region)
Question:
Was legacy-JWT enforcement enabled on our project, or is this a platform-side bug rejecting valid legacy HS256 JWTs at the Edge Functions gateway? If it's intended deprecation, what's the safe migration path; if it's a bug, can it be restored? This is affecting production.

title: Edge Functions show "ACTIVE" but return 404 NOT_FOUND_FUNCTION_BLOB on all requests (49 functions simultaneously after GitHub auto-deploy)
author: victorafael26
body:
# Bug report

<!-- 

⚠️ We receive a lot of bug reports which have already been solved or discussed. If you are looking for help, please try these first:

- Docs: https://docs.supabase.com
- Discussions: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions
- Discord: https://discord.supabase.com

Before opening a bug report, please verify the following: 

-->

- [x] I confirm this is a bug with Supabase, not with my own application. 
- [x] I confirm I have searched the [Docs](https://docs.supabase.com), GitHub [Discussions](https://github.com/supabase/supabase/discussions), and [Discord](https://discord.supabase.com). 

## Describe the bug

After merging a PR to `main` (which triggers the native GitHub↔Supabase auto-deploy integration, not a custom GitHub Actions workflow), 49 Edge Functions in the same project started returning 404 on every request — including CORS preflight `OPTIONS` — even though the Functions dashboard and the Management API (`list_edge_functions`) showed them all as `status: "ACTIVE"` with a recent `updated_at`.

The response reveals an undocumented error code:

```
HTTP/2 404
sb-error-code: NOT_FOUND_FUNCTION_BLOB
sb-gateway-version: 1
{"code":"NOT_FOUND_FUNCTION_BLOB","message":"Requested function was not found"}
```

This differs from the documented generic `NOT_FOUND` code in https://supabase.com/docs/guides/troubleshooting/edge-function-404-error-response — this one specifically references a missing "blob" (bundle), while the function *metadata* clearly exists and is marked active.

**The only fix that worked:** manually running `supabase functions deploy <function-name>` for each affected function (zero code changes) immediately restored it to 200/204. This strongly suggests the function's metadata record was updated to ACTIVE, but the underlying bundle/blob was never correctly persisted or became orphaned during the deploy.

This looks closely related to #35426, which describes the same "shows ACTIVE, 404s anyway, fixed only by manual redeploy" pattern — but that issue never captured the exact `NOT_FOUND_FUNCTION_BLOB` error code, nor this scale (49 functions failing at once from a single deploy event).

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior, please provide code snippets or a repository:

We don't have a reliable on-demand repro yet (issue #35426 also couldn't reproduce on demand), but here is the exact timeline of our incident:

1. Project has ~90 Edge Functions declared across `supabase/config.toml`.
2. A PR is merged into `main` via GitHub (native Supabase↔GitHub integration triggers deploy — not a custom Action).
3. Within minutes, dozens of *unrelated* functions (no code/dependency overlap with the merged PR) start returning `NOT_FOUND_FUNCTION_BLOB` on every request.
4. The Functions Management API (`list_edge_functions`) shows all of them as `ACTIVE`, with `updated_at` timestamps matching the deploy window.
5. Manually redeploying each affected function via CLI (`supabase functions deploy <name>`) fixes it — no code changes made.

## Expected behavior

A deploy (automatic or manual) should be atomic per function: either the function is fully deployed and servable, or the deploy fails visibly and the previous working version stays servable. It should never end up in a state where the metadata says `ACTIVE` but the runtime can't find the underlying bundle.

## Screenshots

N/A — happy to share redacted request/response logs on request (didn't want to paste project-identifying details in a public issue).

## System information

- OS: macOS (client-side CLI usage; the actual bug is server-side and unrelated to client OS)
- Browser (if applies): N/A (server-to-server / Edge Function requests)
- Version of supabase-js: N/A (not a client SDK issue — Edge Functions runtime/deploy pipeline issue)
- Version of Node.js: N/A
- Supabase CLI version: 2.107.0
- Project region: sa-east-1 (South America)
- Deploy method: native GitHub integration (auto-deploy on merge to `main`), NOT custom GitHub Actions
- Runtime: Deno Edge Functions, `edge_runtime.policy = "oneshot"` (per `config.toml`)

## Additional context

- Incident started: 2026-07-07 20:10:01 UTC (17:10:01 America/Sao_Paulo)
- Likely trigger: merge of a PR into `main` ~9 minutes earlier (20:01:28 UTC), which should have kicked off the native GitHub auto-deploy for the whole `supabase/functions/` tree
- Affected: 49 out of ~90 Edge Functions in the project, seemingly at random — no shared code, imports, or dependency between the affected functions
- Resolved by: manual `supabase functions deploy <name>` per function, one at a time (~1h total to redeploy all 49)
- Cross-referencing: #35426 (closed without root cause, same "ACTIVE but 404, fixed only by manual redeploy" pattern)
- Also similar in *symptom only* (not confirmed same cause) to Discussions #26818 (bundle size limit — different mechanism) and #36109 (mass function outage — confirmed root cause there was a `supabase-js` dependency version bug, not applicable here since we made zero dependency changes)
- Happy to share the exact project ref and full timestamped logs (`sb-request-id`, `cf-ray`, function IDs) privately if it helps the Supabase team correlate against internal deploy logs.

title: Update Sentry monitoring guide for the built-in Sentry SDK integration
author: andreiborza
body:
# Improve documentation

## Link

https://supabase.com/docs/guides/telemetry/sentry-monitoring

## Describe the problem

The guide uses the third-party `@supabase/sentry-js-integration` package, but Sentry now ships Supabase support natively in the JavaScript SDK (v9.14.0+). The documented API, `supabaseIntegration(SupabaseClient, Sentry, { tracing, breadcrumbs, errors })`, is incompatible with current `@sentry/*`, so the snippets no longer work as written.

## Describe the improvement

Update the snippets to the built-in API: `Sentry.supabaseIntegration({ supabaseClient })` or `Sentry.instrumentSupabaseClient(client)`. Simplify the Next.js setup to a single instrumentation call, fix the span deduplication example, and note the v9.14.0 requirement (with the community package as the fallback for v7).

## Additional context

Verified against `@sentry/node` + `@supabase/supabase-js`. PR ready to link.
